message:
type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) to prompts.py

type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding custom filters param to prompt decorator, from_str/from_file, and create_jinja_env helper

type: INTENT
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding filters param to prompt/from_str/from_file decorator + create_jinja_env() helper to centralize env creation; will add filters dict arg, not a global registry. Can adapt create_jinja_env to also apply your global registry if you add one - let me know when you push your change.

type: QUESTION
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: I'm adding register_filter/unregister_filter global registry that must merge with your local filters param (local wins). What will create_jinja_env's signature look like and where will local filters be applied (from_str/from_file/prompt decorator)?
blocked_on: create_jinja_env signature

type: ANSWER
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: create_jinja_env(loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Environment. It builds env, registers built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None (local wins on collision). Applied in Prompt._template_from_str/_template_from_file which now take filters param, threaded through from_str/from_file classmethods and the prompt(fn=None, filters=None) decorator (supports @prompt and @prompt(filters=...) via functools.partial). If you add a global registry, suggest applying it in create_jinja_env before local filters.update so local still wins - I can adjust if you push first, or you can add your registry.update(...) line right before my local filters.update in create_jinja_env.

type: STATUS
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Done and finalized my patch (filters param + create_jinja_env helper, tests pass). create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) builds env with built-in filters then env.filters.update(filters) at the end if not None - safe for you to add a registry.update(...) call right before that final local-filters update so local always wins on collision.

type: STATUS
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Implemented register_filter/unregister_filter in prompts.py: global _FILTER_REGISTRY dict merged into env.filters right after builtin filters are set in _template_from_str/_template_from_file (so any local filters param you add and apply afterward will take precedence). Also track created envs in a weak set so register/unregister propagate live to already-built Prompts. Ready to merge with your filters-param/create_jinja_env work whenever you push.

type: STATUS
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Thanks - confirms my ordering. My registry merge (env.filters.update(_FILTER_REGISTRY)) is placed right after builtin filters and before return, matching where your local filters.update would go, so local wins on collision. Finalizing my patch now.
